articles.title: Uncertainty Over Qatar Diplomacy Clouds Prospects For U.S.-Iran Deal
articles.content:
多哈/迪拜6月30日（路透社）——伊朗周二表示，不会与在冲突爆发后飞赴该地区的美国高级特使举行会谈，这为两国之间持久和平的前景蒙上阴影。

伊朗官员还表示，双方仍需首先解决两周前签署的停火协议条款，然后才能讨论更棘手的问题，例如对其核计划的可能限制。

这些事态发展表明，双方在初始框架的几个关键问题上存在重大分歧。该框架要求伊朗解除对霍尔木兹海峡的封锁，以换取经济激励，并设定60天谈判期以敲定永久和平协议。

美国总统唐纳德·特朗普的女婿贾里德·库什纳和特使史蒂夫·威特科夫抵达多哈，白宫称之为“高级别”会谈，但伊朗和东道主卡塔尔表示，他们只会与调解人而非伊朗人本身会面。

卡塔尔表示，总理谢赫·穆罕默德·本·阿卜杜拉赫曼·阿勒萨尼等人与威特科夫和库什纳举行了会晤。

伊朗外交部发言人埃斯马伊尔·巴盖伊表示：“未来几天未安排任何级别与美国方面的会晤。”

据卡塔尔外交部发言人马吉德·安萨里称，两国本应开始较低级别的技术磋商。

据《华尔街日报》援引美国知情人士报道，特朗普正在权衡重返全面战争的选择，与国防部长皮特·赫格塞斯和参谋长联席会议主席丹·卡恩将军讨论实施更多打击。但路透社无法立即证实该报道。

不过，报道称，目前特朗普——他公开威胁要对伊朗发动更多攻击——已决定给外交更多时间。

自2月28日战争爆发以来，霍尔木兹海峡的航运已部分恢复，该海峡战前承载全球五分之一的石油和液化天然气运输。

但伊朗官员表示，他们有权与美军盟友阿曼——这条战略水道另一侧的国家——共同管理交通，并将在60天期限届满时的8月中旬收取通行费。

伊朗首席谈判代表穆罕默德·巴格尔·加里巴夫在国家电视台表示：“霍尔木兹海峡的主权属于伊朗和阿曼，海峡交通须遵守伊朗规定的安排。”

美国副总统JD·万斯表示，将阻止伊朗对这个国际水道收取通行费，他在《迈克尔·诺尔斯节目》中表示：“这不会以伊朗对过境霍尔木兹海峡的船只收取通行费告终。”

万斯在周一录制、周二发布的采访中还表示，霍尔木兹海峡的石油流量已恢复至战前水平，甚至在某些日子超过该水平，但未提供具体数字。

尽管存在不确定性，自周末以来油价已下跌。当时美国轰炸伊朗军事设施以回应无人机对商业船只的袭击，伊朗则攻击了美国在科威特和巴林的军事基地。

但联合国贸易和发展会议周二表示，脆弱经济体可能仍面临食品和燃料价格上涨的风险，即使能源市场感受到缓解之后亦是如此。

这场战争推高了全球通胀，并在11月中期选举前给特朗普带来政治压力，中期选举将决定美国国会的控制权。特朗普和财政部长斯科特·贝森特都在敦促加油站降低价格。

美国与伊朗之间的临时协议还规定结束以色列与伊朗支持的黎巴嫩真主党武装组织在黎巴嫩的冲突。

但真主党盟友、黎巴嫩有权势的议会议长纳比赫·贝里对黎巴嫩与以色列之间另一项由美国促成的停战框架协议表示质疑。

分析师表示，该协议存在固化僵局的风险，因为它将以色列从黎巴嫩南部撤军与真主党解除武装挂钩。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

DOHA/DUBAI, June 30 (Reuters) - Iran said on Tuesday it would not meet with top U.S. envoys who flew to the region following an outbreak of hostilities, clouding the prospects for a lasting peace between the two countries.

Iranian officials also said the two sides must still sort out the terms of a ceasefire they signed two weeks ago before they could tackle more difficult topics, such as possible limits to its nuclear program.

Advertisement

The developments indicated the two sides are far apart on key pillars of the initial framework, which calls for Iran to lift its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for financial incentives, and sets up 60 days of negotiations to work out a permanent peace deal.

U.S. President Donald Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner and envoy Steve Witkoff arrived in Doha for what the White House described as “high level” talks, but Iran and host Qatar said they would meet with mediators, rather than the Iranians themselves.

Qatar said Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman al-Thani was among those to meet with Witkoff and Kushner.

Advertisement

“No meeting at any level with the American side has been scheduled for the coming days,” Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei said.

The two countries were due to commence lower-level technical talks, according to Majed al-Ansari, spokesperson for Qatar’s foreign ministry.

Advertisement

Trump Said To Be Contemplating Strikes

Looking for ways to break the deadlock, Trump has weighed a return to all-out war, holding conversations with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff General Dan Caine on conducting more strikes, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing U.S. sources familiar with the discussion. Reuters could not immediately confirm the report.

But for now Trump, who has publicly threatened Iran with more attacks, has decided to give diplomacy more time, the report said.

Shipping has partially resumed through the strait, which handled one-fifth of global oil and liquefied natural gas before the war broke out on February 28.

Advertisement

But Iranian officials said they had a right to manage traffic along with U.S. ally Oman, which lies on the other side of the strategic waterway, and would impose tolls in mid-August when the 60-day period expires.

“The sovereignty of the Strait of Hormuz lies with Iran and Oman, and traffic in the Strait is subject to arrangements determined by Iran,” Iran’s top negotiator, Mohammed Baqer Qalibaf, said on state TV.

U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Iran would be prevented from charging tolls through the international waterway, telling The Michael Knowles Show, “This is not going to end in a place where the Iranians are collecting tolls on ships going through the Strait of Hormuz.”

Advertisement

Vance also said in the interview recorded on Monday but released on Tuesday that oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz had returned to pre-war levels, even exceeding that on some days, without citing figures.

Despite the uncertainty, oil prices have fallen since the weekend, when the U.S. bombed Iranian military facilities in response to drone strikes on commercial ships and Iran attacked U.S. military sites in Kuwait and Bahrain.

Advertisement

Vulnerable economies, however, could remain at risk from food and fuel price increases even after energy markets feel relief, the U.N. trade and development agency said on Tuesday.

The war pushed up global inflation and has put Trump under political pressure before midterm elections in November that will determine control of the U.S. Congress. Trump and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent are both urging gasoline retailers to lower prices.

The interim deal between the U.S. and Iran also provides for an end to the conflict between Israel and Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah in Lebanon.

Advertisement

But Lebanon’s powerful parliament speaker Nabih Berri, an ally of Hezbollah, cast doubt on a separate, U.S.-brokered framework deal between Lebanon and Israel to halt that war.

Analysts said the deal risks entrenching a stalemate by tying Israel’s withdrawal from southern Lebanon to Hezbollah’s disarmament.

Advertisement

Related

qatar

articles.title: Trump Admin Deported Migrants To Venezuela. Now More Than 100 Are Missing.
articles.content:
迈阿密（美联社讯）——据幸存者称，刚刚被美国驱逐出境的100多人被安置在一家酒店，地震发生时委内瑞拉发生地震，引发了一场搜救行动，寻找被埋在废墟中的幸存者和遇难者。

一架从迈阿密起飞的驱逐航班在周三地震发生前几小时抵达委内瑞拉。根据追踪驱逐航班的“人权优先”组织旗下ICE航班监测项目数据显示，航班上共有146名委内瑞拉人，其中包括19名女性和7名儿童。他们被送往拉瓜伊拉的一家酒店。

58岁的利斯贝斯·波蒂洛说，她与其他约20名被驱逐者从酒店废墟中逃出，在街上寻找帮助。他们看到人们四处奔跑，一些人赤身裸体，一些人赤脚逃出拉瓜伊拉的建筑废墟。该地区是周三7.2级和7.5级地震中受灾最严重的地区之一。

波蒂洛在委内瑞拉马拉开波的家中接受电话采访时说：“我们走了大约五公里，我一路哭个不停……没有通讯信号。”

他们到达了国民警卫队大楼，在那里有机会联系亲属。

波蒂洛停顿了一下，哭泣着说：“我获得了重生；上帝给了我第二次机会。我受到了创伤。”

委内瑞拉政府表示，地震造成超过1700人死亡。

他们被美国驱逐的当天幸存了这场地震。

波蒂洛卷入了特朗普政府的大规模驱逐行动。今年5月，ICE航班监测项目追踪到288架驱逐航班飞往38个国家，包括布基纳法索、柬埔寨、喀麦隆、智利和科特迪瓦。

根据ICE航班监测数据，美国5月份向委内瑞拉运行了12架驱逐航班，每周运营三天。驱逐航班于2025年2月恢复，此前暂停了13个月。

波蒂洛说，政府将他们带到拉瓜伊拉圣殿酒店，他们在那里接受了体检并获得了身份证件。他们被告知第二天可以回家。

波蒂洛与另外16名女性同住在二楼的一个房间。她走到阳台上看向大海，看到天空一片漆黑，天气非常炎热。她回到房间，躺在床上，开始感到自己在被摇晃。

“我开始听到'爸爸，爸爸爸爸爸'的喊声，我看到旁边的女人们开始倒下，”她描述着地震时的声音。“她们都在尖叫求救。”

紧接着，第二次地震来袭。

“我摔倒了，被一根横梁埋住并覆盖，但震动把我被埋的地方移开了，我得以出来，”满身淤青的波蒂洛说。

美国移民与海关执法局尚未立即回应美联社的置评请求。

委内瑞拉政府在社交媒体上发布的一段视频显示，被驱逐者在周三抵达加拉加斯机场时受到委内瑞拉当局的接待。

24岁的珍妮·罗德里格斯告诉Telemundo网络，她当时在那个航班上，被带到了那家酒店。

“我被困在废墟下。一位同事——同班航班的乘客——经过，我设法从碎片中挣脱出一只手，抓住他的裤子，恳求帮助，”她说。“感谢上帝——也感谢他——我得以出来。”

Liliana Rojas告诉Telemundo，她一直在试图寻找她33岁的伴侣。关押他的德克萨斯州埃尔帕索拘留中心只告知他已被驱逐。

“没有人对任何事情给出答复，”罗德里格斯说。

女性表示感觉“重生”幸存

波蒂洛于2021年11月穿越美墨边境进入美国，她说自己有一个待决的庇护申请，但她记不住孩子们的电话号码。她打电话给在美国的丈夫。

“我对他说，'凯撒，我还活着。救救我。'我的丈夫一直说，'不可能。'”她说。“'我还活着，我从废墟中出来了，我还活着，'我告诉他。”

她的丈夫给孩子们打了电话，他们接到了她，得以在第二天晚上与母亲团聚。

“我在那天重生了；24日，我获得了第二次生命，”在佛罗里达州南部生活了四年多的波蒂洛说。

——

此版本更正标题，说明酒店位于拉瓜伊拉。
articles.summary:
MIAMI (AP) — More than 100 people just deported from the United States were being held in a hotel when earthquakes struck Venezuela, setting off a scramble to find survivors and bodies buried in the rubble, according to survivors.

A deportation flight from Miami arrived in Venezuela hours before Wednesday’s earthquakes. On board were 146 Venezuelans, including 19 women and seven children, according to ICE Flight Monitor, an initiative of Human Rights First, which tracks deportation flights. They were transported to a hotel in La Guaira.

Advertisement

Lisbeth Portillo, 58, said she escaped the rubble from the hotel with about 20 other deportees who walked the streets looking for help. They saw people running, some naked and others barefoot as they emerged from the rubble of the building in La Guaira, one of the areas that was hardest hit in Wednesday’s 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes.

“We walked about five kilometers, and I cried and cried … there was no communication,” Portillo said in a phone interview from her home in Maracaibo, Venezuela.

They reached a National Guard building, where they had a chance to call relatives.

“I was born again; God gave me a second chance,” said Portillo. “I am traumatized,” she said after a pause, weeping.

Advertisement

The Venezuelan government says more than 1,700 people were killed.

They survived the earthquake the same day that were deported from the U.S.

Portillo was caught up in the Trump administration’s drive for mass deportations. In May, ICE Flight Monitor tracked 288 deportation flights to 38 countries, including Burkina Faso, Cambodia, Cameroon, Chile and the Ivory Coast.

The U.S. ran 12 deportation flights to Venezuela in May, operating three days a week, according to ICE Flight Monitor. Deportation flights to Venezuela resumed in February 2025 after a 13-month pause.

Portillo said the government took them to the Hotel Santuario La Llanada, where they underwent medical exams and got identification documents. They were told they would go home the next day.

Advertisement

Portillo was staying in a second floor room with 16 other women. She stepped onto a balcony to look at the sea and saw that the sky was black; it was very hot. She returned to the room, laid on a bed, and began to feel herself being shaken.

“I started hearing ‘papa, papa papapa,’, and I saw the women next to me start to fall,” she said, describing the sounds from the earthquake. “They were all screaming for help.”

And almost immediately, the second earthquake.

“I fall and end up buried and covered by a beam, but the shaking shifted everything where I was buried and I was able to get out,” said Portillo, who has bruises all over her body.

Advertisement

U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement did not immediately respond to a request for information from the AP.

A video from the Venezuelan government posted on social media showed images of the deportees being received by Venezuelan authorities upon their arrival at the Caracas airport on Wednesday.

Jenny Rodriguez, 24, told the Telemundo network that she was on the flight and taken to the hotel.

“I was trapped under the rubble. A colleague who had been on the same flight came by; I managed to free my hand from the debris, grabbed him by the trousers, and begged for help”, she said. “Thanks to God — and to him — I was able to get out of there.”

Advertisement

Liliana Rojas told Telemundo that she has been trying to locate her 33-year-old partner. The detention center where he was held in El Paso, Texas, says only told that he was deported.

“No one is giving an answer about anything,” Rojas said.

Woman says she feels ‘born again’ after surviving

Portillo, who crossed the U.S. border with Mexico in November 2021 and said had an pending asylum claim, couldn’t remember her children’s phone number. She called her husband in the United States.

“I said to him, ‘Cesar, I’m alive. Help me.’ And my husband kept saying, ‘It can’t be,’” she said. “‘I’m alive, I made it out of the rubble, I’m alive,’ I told him.”

Advertisement

Her husband called their children, who picked her up and were able to reunite with their mother the following night.

“I was born that day; on the 24th, I was born again,” said Portillo, who lived in South Florida for more than four years.

___

This version corrects the headline to say the hotel was in La Guaira.

Advertisement
